name: mesos_master
explanation: Mesos Master exposes an unauthenticated HTTP REST API on the well-known port 5050. The check GETs /state (cluster topology, version) and /metrics/snapshot (counter snapshot) and parses JSON. Both endpoints are universal across Mesos versions and require no credentials in the default deployment. A generic http-path-probe to GET /state on port 5050 (verifying a JSON body with a 'version' key and Mesos-shaped fields like 'leader' / 'activated_slaves') is sufficient. DC/OS and HTTP-auth-enabled clusters need an auth_token, but those are not the typical case.
notes: Pattern B (single canonical HTTP path). DC/OS deployments require auth_token (file or dcos_auth) but that is the minority case.
